name: QBR Auto-Prep - Backstory Starter
description: Automatically prepares quarterly business review materials for every account on an upcoming QBR agenda. The workflow scans the calendar for meetings tagged as QBRs (or matching configurable title patterns), then for each account on the agenda, pulls the full quarter's engagement data from Backstory: meeting frequency, email volume, contacts engaged, key relationship changes, and deal progression. An AI agent generates a structured QBR prep document with executive summary, engagement trends, wins/risks, and talking points. Delivered to the account team 48 hours before the QBR.

nodes.name: Workflow Parameters
nodes.notes: Fetch calendar meetings tagged as QBRs within the next 48 hours and capture linked account names. Replace the placeholder API endpoints and tokens with real systems before enabling this workflow.

nodes.name: Get Backstory Auth Token
nodes.notes: Optional if you are using MCP only, but kept here to match the existing repo’s n8n patterns.

nodes.name: Fetch Source Records
nodes.notes: Fetch calendar meetings tagged as QBRs within the next 48 hours and capture linked account names.

nodes.name: Normalize Source Records
nodes.notes: Converts raw upcoming QBR meetings responses into one item per record for downstream AI analysis.

nodes.name: Analyze QBR Auto-Prep

parameters.text:
=You are generating the QBR Auto-Prep workflow output.

PURPOSE:
Automatically prepares quarterly business review materials for every account on an upcoming QBR agenda. The workflow scans the calendar for meetings tagged as QBRs (or matching configurable title patterns), then for each account on the agenda, pulls the full quarter's engagement data from Backstory: meeting frequency, email volume, contacts engaged, key relationship changes, and deal progression. An AI agent generates a structured QBR prep document with executive summary, engagement trends, wins/risks, and talking points. Delivered to the account team 48 hours before the QBR.

SOURCE PAYLOAD:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.record, null, 2) }}

TASKS:
1. Use Backstory MCP to enrich the record with the most relevant account, opportunity, engagement, or stakeholder context.
2. Decide what matters most for the qbr auto-prep use case.
3. Produce a concise markdown summary with: headline, key findings, risks, recommended actions, and routing notes if applicable.

OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS:
- Keep it compact enough for Slack.
- Prefer bullets.
- Mention concrete dates, owners, amounts, or stages when the data provides them.
- If the source record is incomplete, explicitly say what is missing.

REFERENCE STYLE:
📑 **QBR Prep Document** — ACME Corp | Meeting: Thursday Mar 13, 2:00 PM

📊 **EXECUTIVE SUMMARY:**
- Account: ACME Corp | ARR: ===$425,000=== | Customer since Jan 2024
- Health Score: 8/10 (up from 7 last quarter)
- Quarter highlights: 2 new departments onboarded, feature adoption up 23%
- Primary risk: Executive sponsor engagement declining (see below)

📈 **QUARTER-OVER-QUARTER TRENDS:**
- Meetings: 18 this quarter vs 14 last quarter (+29%)

options.systemMessage: You are a Backstory workflow analyst generating QBR Auto-Prep outputs. Keep the response actionable, concrete, and Slack-friendly.

nodes.notes: Wire your preferred LLM credentials to the chat model node after import.

nodes.name: Backstory MCP
nodes.notes: This mirrors the existing workflow infrastructure in the repo. Add MCP auth headers after import.

nodes.name: Aggregate Results
nodes.notes: Collapses all analyzed records into a single outbound summary. Replace this with per-owner routing if you want one message per rep/CSM/executive.

nodes.name: Post to Slack
nodes.notes: Swap this node for Teams or SMTP if Slack is not your delivery target.
